data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the project. Let me plan the migration from xUnit.net v2 to v3:

1. **TestProject.csproj changes:**
   - Replace `xunit` v2 package with `xunit.v3` (the v3 package)
   - Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (v3 uses `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3 or the built-in runner)
   - Remove `xunit.runner.visualstudio` (v3 projects are executables, not class libraries)
   - Remove `Xunit.SkippableFact` — xUnit v3 has built-in skip support
   - Switch the output type to `Exe` since v3 test projects run as standalone executables, and update the target framework if necessary

2. **ConditionalTests.cs changes:**
   - xUnit v3 replaces the `SkippableFact` and `Skip.IfNot()` pattern with built-in skip support using `Assert.Skip()` directly in the test method or a `Skip` parameter on the `[Fact]` attribute, so I'll convert the conditional skip logic to use these native mechanisms instead.

Now I'm verifying the exact xUnit v3 skip API—it looks like `Assert.Skip()` handles unconditional skips, while `Assert.SkipWhen()` and `Assert.SkipUnless()` handle conditional skipping based on boolean conditions.

I should also check whether the global.json configuration needs updates for xUnit v3, since the test runner setup might differ from v2. me think about this...

Actually, in xUnit v3, the project becomes an executable, and you can run tests with `dotnet run` or `dotnet test`. The global.json with `"test": {"runner": "VSTest"}` might not be needed or might need updating.

Actually wait, xUnit v3 ships its own runner. The recommended way is:
- For `dotnet test`, you still need the Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk but xUnit v3 works differently
- Actually, xUnit v3 projects are self-contained executables. You run them directly or via `dotnet run`.
- But `dotnet test` still works if you use the proper setup.

Let me reconsider. For xUnit v3:

The csproj should be:
- OutputType: Exe
- Remove Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk

So I need to swap out the xunit package for xunit.v3, remove the test SDK and visualstudio runner, and drop the SkippableFact dependency. Since xUnit v3 is self-contained with its own built-in runner, the global.json VSTest configuration is probably unnecessary now. I need to figure out the right xUnit v3 package version to use for this migration, so let me check what's currently available on NuGet rather than guessing.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://www.nuget.org/packages/xunit.v3:
*   [README](#readme-body-tab)
*   [Frameworks](#supportedframeworks-body-tab)
*   [Dependencies](#dependencies-body-tab)
*   [Used By](#usedby-body-tab)
*   [Versions](#versions-body-tab)
*   [Release Notes](#releasenotes-body-tab)

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). It is licensed under [Apache 2](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) (an OSI approved license). The project is [governed](https://xunit.net/governance) by a Project Lead.

For project documentation, please visit the [xUnit.net project home](https://xunit.net/).

*   _New to xUnit.net? Get started with the [.NET SDK](https://xunit.net/docs/getting-started/v3/getting-started)._
*   _Need some help building the source? See [BUILDING.md](https://github.com/xunit/xunit/tree/main/BUILDING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the project? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/xunit/.github/tree/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)._
*   _Want to contribute to the assertion library? See the [suggested contribution workflow](https://github.com/xunit/assert.xunit/tree/main/README.md#suggested-contribution-workflow) in the assertion library project, as it is slightly more complex due to code being spread across two GitHub repositories._

[![Powered by NDepend](https://raw.github.com/xunit/media/main/powered-by-ndepend-transparent.png)](http://www.ndepend.com/)

### Latest Builds

Latest CI ([how to use](https://xunit.net/docs/using-ci-builds))

data.content:
Good, latest is `3.2.2`. Now let me apply all the migration changes:

1. **`.csproj`**: Switch to `Exe`, replace packages with `xunit.v3`, remove `Xunit.SkippableFact` (v3 has built-in skip), remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `xunit.runner.visualstudio`
2. **`ConditionalTests.cs`**: Replace `SkippableFact`/`SkippableTheory` + `Skip.If`/`Skip.IfNot` with v3's built-in `Assert.SkipWhen`/`Assert.SkipUnless`
3. **`global.json`**: Remove obsolete VSTest runner config (v3 uses Microsoft Testing Platform)
